data.title: 11 internal wallets dumped LIBRA and made a profit of $43.8 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to LookonChain monitoring, 11 internal wallets made a profit of $43.8 million by selling LIBRA.
These wallets were created a few hours before the launch of LIBRA, and the corresponding funds for purchasing LIBRA were also prepared in advance.

data.title: Magic Eden: Unclaimed ME tokens have been airdropped to stakers’ wallets
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Magic Eden official posted on the X platform: Users can check their wallets, and the addresses participating in the pledge will receive a share of the unclaimed ME token airdrop. You can go to http://magiceden.io/earn to pledge, or pledge directly from your Magic Eden mobile app token details page.

data.title: Argentine President explains why he deleted his tweet: He does not understand the token project and has nothing to do with it
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Argentine President Javier Milei posted on the X platform: "A few hours ago, I posted a tweet, as I have posted many times before, supporting a so-called private enterprise, but obviously I have nothing to do with the enterprise. I don’t know the details of the project. After learning about it, I decided not to continue to spread the news (that’s why I deleted the tweet)."

data.title: CZ tweeted that he would no longer post any more dog-related tweets
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Binance founder CZ posted on the X platform: "Alright, no more dog tweets."

data.title: DOGE: 586 contracts with 10 institutions that wasted money have been terminated
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to the official account of the US government efficiency department DOGE, it has cooperated with 10 agencies to terminate 586 contracts that waste taxpayer funds, including a USDA contract worth $8.2 million for "implementing environmental compliance services for pilot projects developed under the Climate Smart Commodity Partnership." The tweet was retweeted by Musk.

data.title: A certain address spent $1.1 million to buy 5.1 million LIBRA in the early stage, with a return rate of 545%
data.description: According to Odaily Planet Daily, according to The Data Nerd, an address spent 1.1 million USDC to buy 5.1 million LIBRA after the suspected Argentine President Javier Milei released the LIBRA contract. Afterwards, the address sold 4.6 million tokens for $7.1 million, realizing a profit of $6 million, with a return on investment of 545%. Currently, the address only holds 500,000 tokens, equivalent to about $283,000.

data.title: BitMart is about to list LIBRA (LIBRA)
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, BitMart will launch LIBRA (LIBRA) at 12:00 (Eastern Time) on February 15, and the LIBRA/USDT trading pair will be opened. It is reported that LIBRA is the Solana ecosystem Meme coin.

data.title: Grayscale transferred out about 1,250 BTC and 1,357 ETH in the early morning, with a total value of over $127 million
The U.S. Senate accused Pantera's founder of cashing out more than $1 billion from immigrants and failing to pay taxes properly
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to US media reports, the Senate Finance Committee is investigating whether Pantera Capital founder Dan Morehead violated federal tax laws after moving to the tax haven of Puerto Rico. 
According to a letter released by the Senate Finance Committee, Morehead sold "substantial shares" after moving to Puerto Rico and obtained "more than $1 billion" in capital gains. The committee asked Morehead to share information related to these transactions, including the name of his tax advisor. The letter also asked him to provide a list of all assets he sold while living in Puerto Rico, including cryptocurrencies. 
Morehead said in a statement that he moved to Puerto Rico in 2021 and believed that he had taken appropriate actions in paying taxes. A spokesman for Oregon Democratic Senator Ron Wyden said the investigation is ongoing and declined to comment further. (New York Times)

data.title: An insider spent $5.75 million to buy 2.29 million LIBRA, and currently has a floating loss of $4.34 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Onchain Lens monitoring, an insider address spent 28,740 SOL (worth $5.75 million) to purchase 2.29 million LIBRA and is currently facing a floating loss of $4.34 million. The wallet also received 650,000 LIBRA from the contract deployer wallet and transferred it to another address.
